items.hook: Gracie Abrams captures pain of early adulthood in new album - but you're left wanting more
items.body: The singer's third album is full of incisive, witty lyrics that are let down by muted production.

items.hook: At least seven people are killed and 32 others are injured after a bomb explodes on an Israeli tour bus at Burgas Airport, Bulgaria.
items.body: The 2012 Burgas bus bombing was a terrorist attack carried out by a suicide bomber on a passenger bus transporting Israeli tourists at the Burgas Airport in Burgas, Bulgaria, on 18 July 2012. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse - Konami classic rises again from Paris sewers and Joan of Arc is a boss
items.body:
Evil Empire creatives explain how it is playing to today’s ‘metroidvanias’ and honouring the original’s legacy with much fresh slaying to be done

Since the last Castlevania game hit the shelves (2014’s

items.hook: 16 World Cup viral moments - from Posh Spice's calm reaction to Haaland's bromance
items.body: We look at some of the most memorable scenes during the 2026 World Cup that set the internet alight.

items.hook: Gemma Files is a Canadian horror writer, journalist, and film critic.
items.body: Her short story, "The Emperor's Old Bones", won the International Horror Guild Award for Best Short Story of 1999. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Sack of Aquileia: After an earlier defeat on the Catalaunian Plains, Attila lays siege to the metropolis of Aquileia and eventually destroys it.
items.body: The Sack of Aquileia occurred in 452, and was carried out by the Huns under the leadership of Attila. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Glassriver and Network Movie team up on Áróra TV drama adaptation - Production / Funding - Iceland/Germany
items.body: The Icelandic-German production partnership is adapting Lilja Sigurðardóttir’s bestselling crime novel series into an international TV drama set across Iceland and London

items.hook: Add to playlist: the nervy breakbeats and acid delirium of Silverwingkiller and the week’s best new tracks
items.body:
The industrial dance duo make music for this summer’s heatwave: filled with dread and jangling with pent-up energy

From Manchester, via Peterborough and Shanghai
Recommended if you like Crystal

items.hook: The Villa in Tiergarten Park is a 1927 German silent romance film directed by Franz Osten and starring Joe Stöckel, Aud Egede-Nissen, and Elsa Krueger.
items.body: It still survives unlike many films from the silent era. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Abdullah Thabit is a Saudi Arabian poet, novelist and journalist.
items.body: He was born in the city of Abha in the southern province of Asir. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: A gunpowder magazine explosion in Birgu, Malta, kills around 200 people.
items.body: On 18 July 1806, approximately 40,000 lb (18,000 kg) of gunpowder stored in a magazine (polverista) in Birgu, Malta, mistakenly detonated. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: On the Caribbean island of Montserrat, the Soufrière Hills volcano erupts. Over the course of several years, it devastates the island, destroying the capital, forcing most of the population to flee.
items.body: Montserrat is a British Overseas Territory in the Caribbean. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: As of 2009, it has an estimated population of 11,189.
items.body: Busumbala , also known as Old-Busumbala, is a small town in western Gambia. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Giovanni Veronesi’s Dio ride to close the 83rd Venice Film Festival - Venice 2026
items.body: Set in the 17th century and inspired by true events, the Tuscan filmmaker’s new movie starring Pierfrancesco Favino and Silvio Orlando will be presented Out of Competition

items.hook: Sarah Ladipo Manyika FRSL is a British-Nigerian writer.
items.body: She is the author of In Dependence (2009) and Like A Mule Bringing Ice Cream To The Sun (2016). Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Kanuru Heggaditi or Kanooru Heggadithi (meaning: Proprietress of Kanuru) is a Kannada language novel written by author and poet, Kuvempu, in 1936.
items.body: Based on the novel, a Kannada movie Kanooru Heggadithi directed by Girish Karnad, was released in 1999. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Ladj Ly’s Dumas : Diable noir now in post-production - Production / Funding - France
items.body: Théo Christine, François Civil, Vincent Cassel, Omar Sy and Galatéa Bellugi are headlining this SRAB Films and Chapter 2 production, sold worldwide by Goodfellas

items.hook: Roman–Persian Wars: Emperor Julian arrives at Antioch with a Roman expeditionary force (60,000 men) and stays there for nine months to launch a campaign against the Persian Empire.
items.body: The Roman–Persian wars, also called the Roman–Iranian wars, took place between the Greco-Roman world and the Iranian world, beginning with the Roman Republic and the Parthian Empire in 54 BC and ending with the Byzantine and the Sasanian empires in 628 AD. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: A racially charged incident in a bar sparks the six-day Hough riots in Cleveland, Ohio; 1,700 Ohio National Guard troops intervene to restore order.
items.body: The Hough riots were riots in the predominantly African-American community of Hough in Cleveland, Ohio, United States which took place from July 18 to 23, 1966. One of those things you can't un-know.
